pi_items.source: Verified against CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1050) and HazMat Tool database. Train manifest file confirms UN 1050 assigned to TC-101.
CAMEO Chemicals official database and HazMat Tool confirm UN 1050 = Hydrogen chloride, anhydrous (HCl). Manifest and task description consistent.
Verified against CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1005) and HazMat Tool database. Train manifest file confirms UN 1005 assigned to TC-102.
CAMEO Chemicals official database and HazMat Tool confirm UN 1005 = Ammonia, anhydrous. Manifest and task description consistent.
Verified against CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/2209) and HazMat Tool database. Train manifest file confirms UN 2209 assigned to TC-103.

pi_items.verified_value: Formaldehyde solutions, with not less than 25% formaldehyde
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals official database confirms UN 2209 = Formaldehyde solutions (with not less than 25% formaldehyde). Hazmat Tool and ADR 2025 documentation consistent.
Verified against CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1791) and HazMat Tool database. Train manifest file confirms UN 1791 assigned to TC-104.
CAMEO Chemicals official database and HazMat Tool confirm UN 1791 = Hypochlorite solutions. Commonly sodium hypochlorite. Note: can react violently with ammonia or acids.

pi_items.metadata_value: HCl + NH3 → ammonium chloride aerosol + toxic cloud
pi_items.verified_value: HCl(g) + NH3(g) → NH4Cl(s) white aerosol cloud, highly exothermic reaction producing inhalation hazard
pi_items.source: Verified against multiple chemistry databases and RSC Education demonstrations. When hydrogen chloride gas and ammonia gas meet, they form solid ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) as a dense white cloud. The reaction is highly exothermic and the ammonium chloride smoke is an extreme inhalation hazard.
Specified in task.json metadata and referenced in setup_task.sh (line 27) and export_result.sh (line 17). Output file path is set correctly in hooks.

Source file exists at /workspace/data/train_derailment_manifest.txt (verified at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/cameo_chemicals_env/data/train_derailment_manifest.txt). Setup script (line 30) copies it to /home/ga/Desktop/train_derailment_manifest.txt as specified in metadata.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata chemical claims are verified against authoritative sources (CAMEO Chemicals NOAA, HazMat Tool). UN 1050 corresponds to Hydrogen Chloride (anhydrous), UN 1005 to Ammonia (anhydrous), UN 2209 to Formaldehyde solutions (≥25% formaldehyde), and UN 1791 to Hypochlorite solutions. The key reaction claim is chemically accurate: hydrogen chloride gas and ammonia gas react exothermically to form ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) as a dense white aerosol cloud, producing an extreme inhalation hazard. The synthetic train derailment scenario contains a 38-car derailment with 4 tank cars; TC-101 and TC-102 are actively leaking and physically adjacent, creating the classic HCl-ammonia reaction with white cloud formation. The manifest is a synthetic document (Incident ID REMD-2026-0089) but the chemical properties and reaction dynamics are based on real chemistry. All file paths in metadata are correctly specified for the Linux task environment.
